competitors.name: momentum_breakout
competitors.rules: Buy a 20-day-high breakout confirmed by above-average volume; exit when price closes below its 20-day SMA. Few big winners, many small losers.

competitors.name: blended_momo_rsi
competitors.rules: Momentum breakout, but skip entries already overbought (RSI ≥ 70); exit on trend break or RSI > 75. Avoids chasing extended moves.

competitors.name: mean_reversion
competitors.rules: Buy when RSI(14) < 30 (oversold); sell when RSI(14) > 70 (overbought). Trades less, wins more often, smaller edge.

competitors.name: deep_research_analyst
competitors.rules: Holds the analyst's target weights; rebalanced each tick from a fresh research report (web + Robinhood data).

competitors.name: mirofish_swarm
competitors.rules: Allocates across the swarm's top vote-getters (weight ∝ vote share, capped at 60%); rebalanced each tick.

analyst.as_of: 2026-06-20 (weekend; daily bars through 2026-06-18 close, markets closed Fri 6/19 Juneteenth)
analyst.sizing: 4 reasonable-multiple names, ~36% cash buffer into MU's 6/24 print and a hawkish new-chair Fed
analyst.framework: Claude for Financial Services — equity-research methodology (/screen → /sector → /comps → /catalysts → /thesis → portfolio). Data: Robinhood fundamentals + web_search (no paid-vendor MCP connectors).

regime.label: Hawkish new-chair Fed vs. a volatile AI-semis melt-up
regime.note: Kevin Warsh's first FOMC meeting landed hawkish — 9 of 18 officials now pencil in 2026 hikes — and triggered a 'Fed selloff', after a -4% chip-led Nasdaq day on June 4 (worst since Apr-2025). The tape recovered into a fresh semis melt-up (Nasdaq ~26.5k, S&P ~7.5k), but with the whole complex at/near 52-wk highs into a hawkish higher-for-longer signal, the thin air is in the richest long-duration multiples. Favor reasonable-multiple leadership + a real cash buffer over chasing 100–500x parabolas.

analyst.thesis: The screen is one trade: a memory/AI-infrastructure semis melt-up. MU (+62% 1mo, +388% 6mo), ARM (+97% 1mo), MRVL (+76% 1mo) and AMD lead; mega-cap software (MSFT -9%, PLTR -17% 3mo) and China lag. The financial-services lens leans INTO the super-cycle but only through reasonable multiples and refuses the parabolas: ARM at 519x P/E and MRVL at 106x (on a 251M-share blow-off day, 3x its average) are exactly where a hawkish-chair, higher-for-longer turn does the most damage. The book's anchor pick is MU (54x P/E) — the leadership of the memory super-cycle, with HBM reportedly sold out and records guided, reporting 6/24; it is sized for a binary print, not bet on. NVDA is the valuation anomaly — 32x P/E, the cheapest of the AI semis and ~11% below its high while the rest of the complex went vertical, an asymmetric laggard. QCOM (24.6x, 1.6% yield, only mid-range vs its high) is the value-plus-momentum sleeve. GE Aerospace (44x, vol ~2%/day, clean uptrend) is the non-semi diversifier that cuts the book's chip beta. ~36% cash respects a stretched tape at the highs into a hawkish Fed and a binary MU catalyst.

evidence.point: MU 54x P/E, 52-wk range $103→$1,149 (last $1,134, at the high); HBM sold out, records guided, reports 6/24
ARM 519x P/E / MRVL 106x on 251M-share volume (3x avg) — parabolic, screened out on valuation/blow-off risk
NVDA 32x P/E, ~11% below its 52-wk high and -4.5% on the month while peers melted up — cheapest AI-semi, asymmetric laggard
QCOM 24.6x P/E, 1.6% dividend, range-position ~75% (not overextended) — value + momentum
GE Aerospace 44x P/E, 20d realized vol ~2%/day, fresh high — non-semi diversifier and regime hedge
Warsh-Fed first meeting hawkish (9/18 see 2026 hikes), 'Fed selloff' then recovery; markets closed Fri 6/19 Juneteenth

risks: MU's 6/24 print is binary — a soft HBM/guidance read can gap the whole memory complex down; the position is sized for that, but it still dominates near-term P&L.
Momentum keeps ripping: by refusing ARM/MRVL at the highs, the book underperforms if the parabola extends.
A hawkish higher-for-longer turn that actually bites would hit even the 'reasonable' 30–50x multiples here, not just the 500x names.
~36% cash drags if the melt-up broadens; the trade-off is deliberate into the catalyst.

data_examined.label: Screen: 5d/21d/63d/126d momentum, 20d vol, range-position across the 100-name universe
Comps: P/E, P/B, market cap, 52-wk range, dividend on MU/ARM/MRVL/AMD/NVDA/QCOM/GE/HOOD
Regime: Warsh-Fed first-meeting reaction, semis volatility, June-24 MU catalyst

swarm.note: Live swarm — 149 fish, each a unique trader profile, voted via OpenRouter. Independent-voter election; dissenters (ringed) chose CASH.

live.note: Connected to the Agentic cash account •••• — currently unfunded ($0). Deposit into this specific account to trade the real $100. No agent orders placed yet.

rules.momentum_breakout: Buy a 20-day-high breakout confirmed by above-average volume; exit when price closes below its 20-day SMA. Few big winners, many small losers.
rules.mean_reversion: Buy when RSI(14) < 30 (oversold); sell when RSI(14) > 70 (overbought). Trades less, wins more often, smaller edge.
rules.blended_momo_rsi: Momentum breakout, but skip entries already overbought (RSI ≥ 70); exit on trend break or RSI > 75. Avoids chasing extended moves.
rules.deep_research_analyst: Holds the analyst's target weights; rebalanced each tick from a fresh research report (web + Robinhood data).
rules.mirofish_swarm: Allocates across the swarm's top vote-getters (weight ∝ vote share, capped at 60%); rebalanced each tick.

sources.name: Standings, curves, decision trail (LIVE)
sources.detail: The forward paper test — every competitor trades a $100 book the same way, advanced one trading day per tick (rule strategies via tick.py / bot/engine.py; agents via run_agents.py / bot/paper.py), 5 bps slippage, −15% stop. Young — it grows one session per tick.

sources.name: Backtest reference
sources.detail: A separate Dec–Jun walk-forward backtest (2025-12-01–2026-06-18, in-sample, mostly-bull) is shown inside each rule strategy's detail for context only — it is NOT the live board.

sources.name: Research analyst
sources.detail: Agent-driven on the Claude Code plan: web_search + Robinhood data → one report (state/analyst.json) with target weights per tick. Sources link from each evidence row.

sources.name: Swarm vote
sources.detail: Independent-voter election: 150 unique-profile cheap models (DeepSeek/Gemini/Llama/Haiku via OpenRouter) each read one shared briefing and return a single ballot; tallied in bot/swarm.py. No interaction between fish.

sources.name: Live account
sources.detail: Robinhood MCP, Agentic cash account ••••, via get_portfolio / get_equity_positions / get_equity_orders.
